market_question: Will the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) win the most seats in the 2026 West Bengal Legislative Assembly election?
reasoning_summary: Base rate 25% for national party defeating entrenched regional incumbent after re-election → 2021 landslide + consistent double-digit TMC poll lead shift -14 pp → 11% raw, calibration -10 pp → 31% because evidence of BJP momentum is absent and TMC structural advantages (minority vote 30%, female welfare base, rural patronage) remain intact
calibration_applied: Politics category -10 pp applied to final probability because my historical political bets are 25% accurate | hard_cap=68% (overconf 49x) | cat_adj=-10% (politics)
key_factors_for: BJP won 18/42 Lok Sabha seats in WB in 2019 (best ever)
Modi remains popular nationally and BJP has huge money/ground force
Party has been poaching TMC MLAs and building cadre for 5+ years
key_factors_against: TMC won 213/294 Assembly seats in 2021 (super-majority) with 48% vote share
No credible post-2021 poll shows BJP ahead; CVoter Jan-2024 poll gives TMC 46%, BJP 32%
Regional parties in India almost never lose after first re-election; TMC just achieved that in 2021
BJP vote share fell 10 pp between 2019 LS and 2021 LA elections in same seats
Incumbent TMC controls state police & administration and has delivered welfare schemes (Lakshmir Bhandar, Swasthya Sathi)
